items.q: Who owns the code I'm paying you to build?
items.a: You do. Once we're squared away, all the source code, designs, and assets are 100% yours. The only thing I keep is the right to mention I worked on it (and only if that's okay with you).
Happy to. Send yours over, or I can share a simple one-page mutual NDA. Either way, what we talk about stays between us.

items.q: What if we go over budget or scope?
items.a: I quote fixed prices on a fixed scope, so the number we agree on is the number you pay. If you want to add something mid-build, we'll chat about it and I'll give you a clear estimate before any extra work starts. And if I underestimated to begin with, that's on me, the original price still holds.

items.q: Do you do the design too, or do I bring designs?
items.a: Either works! I usually design and build together since it tends to be faster and more cohesive. If you've already got a designer or brand guidelines, I'll happily work with what you have.
Usually 50% up front, 50% on launch. For longer projects we can split the back half into milestone payments, whatever works for your cash flow. Stripe handles the invoices: card, ACH, or wire.

items.q: What if I'm not happy with something?
items.a: Please tell me as soon as you can! I share work weekly so we can catch anything early, while it's still easy to change. If we get to launch and something still isn't right, fixes during the 30-day support window are on me.

items.q: What if I don't know exactly what I want yet?
items.a: That's totally fine, and pretty normal. Bring me whatever you've got, a rough idea, a sketch, a pile of screenshots. We'll figure out the shape of it together on the first call.
